memoryArtifacts.headline: At the beginning of the 12th century Adolf II of Berg donated the site of their old ancestral castle, Schloss Berg, to Cistercian monks from Burgundy. Adolf IV later built the Altenberg Abbey.
The Altenberg Abbey, founded by Adolf II von Berg.
Castle of Altena, built by Adolph IV

zh.lifeSummary: 當Graf Adolf von Berg III於1080年出生在海利格斯 洛米設斯 萊奇賀爾左圖姆 伯格時，他的父親Adolf II. von Berg-Hövel35歲，母親Adelheid von Laufen31歲。他和Irmgard Von Sponheim至少有1個兒子。他於1160年在海因里希_海普洛文次可萊斯 維茲拉爾阿爾騰格被列為mönch。他去世於1160年10月12日之後在德國諾爾德海-維斯特法倫海尼-吉設 可萊斯奧登塔爾阿爾騰格，埋葬在海利格斯 洛米設斯 萊奇庫爾斯騰圖姆 普法爾茲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Graf Adolf von Berg III于1080年出生在海利格斯 洛米設斯 萊奇賀爾左圖姆 伯格时，他的父亲Adolf II. von Berg-Hövel35岁，母亲Adelheid von Laufen31岁。他和Irmgard Von Sponheim至少有1个儿子。他于1160年在海因里希_海普洛文次可萊斯 維茲拉爾阿爾騰格被列为mönch。他去世于1160年10月12日之后在德国諾爾德海-維斯特法倫海尼-吉設 可萊斯奧登塔爾阿爾騰格，埋葬在海利格斯 洛米設斯 萊奇庫爾斯騰圖姆 普法爾茲。

nameMeanings.name: von
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Altered form of Welsh   Vaughan  .
Chinese: variant of   Wong  .
Cambodian: written វ៉ុន, unexplained.

variants.name: Vaughan
meanings: Welsh: from fychan, a lenited form of bychan, a diminutive of bach ‘little’. This was often used to distinguish the younger of two bearers of the same personal name, typically the son of a father with the same name.

nameMeanings.name: Berg
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Erik, Lennart, Bjorn, Lars, Nils, Alf, Nels, Thor, Tor, Knute, Selmer, Anders.

German and Dutch: topographic name for someone who lived on or by a hill or mountain, from Middle High German berc, Middle Dutch berghe. This surname is also found in some other parts of Europe, e.g. in France (Alsace and Lorraine) and Poland. Compare Dutch Vandenberg .
Norwegian and Danish: habitational name from a farmstead named with Old Norse bjarg ‘mountain, hill’.

variants.name: Berger
meanings: German, Dutch, Swedish, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): topographic name for someone who lived in the mountains or hills (see   Berg  ). The surname of German origin is also found in many other European countries, e.g. in France (Alsace and Lorraine), Russia, Poland, Czechia, Hungary, and Croatia, and Slovenia, often as a translation into German of corresponding Slavic topographic names or surnames. As a Jewish name it is mainly artificial. Compare   Bargar  ,   Barger  ,   Barker  ,   Barrier  , and   Barriger  .
French: occupational name from Old French bergier ‘shepherd’ (from Late Latin berbicarius, from berbex ‘ram’). It is also found in England, as a surname of Huguenot origin. Compare Shepard .
Norwegian: habitational name from any of various farms so named with the plural of   Berg   ‘mountain’.

variants.name: Bergh
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Johan, Anders, Arndt, Owe, Per, Sigvard, Sven, Syvert.
Scandinavian: variant of   Berg  .
Dutch and English: topographic name denoting someone living on or by a hill, Middle Dutch berghe, Middle English bergh ‘hill’ (Old English beorg). Compare Dutch Berg and Vandenbergh .

variants.name: Vandenberg
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Dutch Dirk, Marinus, Gerrit, Gerritt, Hendrik, Jacobus, Maarten, Pieter, Wim, Albertus, Durk, Hendrick.
Dutch (Van den Berg) and Flemish (also Van den Berg): topographic name for someone who lived on or by a hill, Middle Dutch berghe. Compare Berg .

variants.name: Berglin
meanings: Swedish: variant of Berg + the suffix -lin, derived from Latin -(l)inius ‘relating to’.

variants.name: Berget
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Knut.
Norwegian: habitational name from any of several farm names especially in eastern Norway named with the definite singular form of berg ‘mountain, hill’ (see Berg ).
French: topographic name, a derivative of   Berge   ‘river bank’.

variants.name: Baerg
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Gerhard, Kurt.
German (Bärg): variant of Berg .

variants.name: Bergen
meanings: German and Jewish (Ashkenazic): habitational name from any of various places called Bergen.
Dutch and Flemish: topographic name for someone who lived on or by a hill (see   Berg  ), or a habitational name from any of various places so named (see   Van Bergen  ).
Swedish (mainly Bergén): ornamental or habitational name formed with the (placename) element berg ‘mountain, hill’ + the adjectival suffix -en/-én, a derivative of Latin -enius ‘relating to’.

variants.name: Berghorst
meanings: Dutch (Gelderland): topographic name for someone living on or by a hill with shrubberies (see   Berg   and   Horst  ).

variants.name: Barg
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Bernhard, Erwin, Hans, Mathias, Wilfried.
North German:
topographic name for someone who lived on a hill, Low German barg. Compare Berg .

variants.name: Berge
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Maren, Johan, Karsten, Alf, Bent, Erik, Gudmund, Knud, Peer, Per, Sten, Thor.
Norwegian and Swedish: variant of   Berg  , from the dative form. As a Norwegian surname, this is a habitational name from any of numerous farms so named all over the country.
German: topographic name for a person by or on a hill, from an extended form of Berg (formerly with a preposition with dative case, e.g. am Berge).

nameMeanings.name: Adolf
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: from Adelwolf, a compound of adal ‘noble’ + wolf ‘wolf’.

en.lifeSummary: When Graf Adolf von Berg III was born in 1080, in Berg, Holy Roman Empire, his father, Adolf II. von Berg-Hövel, was 35 and his mother, Adelheid von Laufen, was 31. He had at least 1 son with Irmgard Von Sponheim. In 1160, at the age of 80, his occupation is listed as mönch in Altenberg, Wetzlar, Rhineland, Prussia. He died after 12 October 1160, in Altenberg, Odenthal, Rheinisch-Bergischer Kreis, North Rhine-Westphalia, Germany, and was buried in Palatinate, Holy Roman Empire.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Graf Adolf von Berg III est né en 1080, à Herzogtum Berg, Saint-Empire romain, son père, Adolf II. von Berg-Hövel, avait 35 ans et sa mère, Adelheid von Laufen, avait 31 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fils avec Irmgard Von Sponheim. En 1160, à l’âge de 80 ans, sa profession de son indiquée est mönch à Altenberg, Kreis Wetzlar, Rhénanie, Prusse. Il est décédé après 12 octobre 1160, à Altenberg, Odenthal, Rheinisch-Bergischer Kreis, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Allemagne, et a été enterré à Kurfürstentum Pfalz, Saint-Empire romain.

de.lifeSummary: Als Graf Adolf von Berg III 1080 in Herzogtum Berg, Heiliges Römisches Reich geboren wurde, war sein Vater Adolf II. von Berg-Hövel 35 und seine Mutter Adelheid von Laufen 31. Er hatte mit Irmgard Von Sponheim mindestens 1 Sohn. 1160, als er 80 Jahre alt war, war sein Beruf als mönch in Altenberg, Kreis Wetzlar, Rheinprovinz, Preußen aufgeführt. Er starb nach 12. Oktober 1160 in Altenberg, Odenthal, Rheinisch-Bergischer Kreis, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Deutschland und wurde in Kurfürstentum Pfalz, Heiliges Römisches Reich begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Graf Adolf von Berg III è nato il nel 1080 nel Herzogtum Berg, Heiliges Römisches Reich, il suo padre, Adolf II. von Berg-Hövel, aveva 35 anni e il suo madre, Adelheid von Laufen, aveva 31 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio con Irmgard Von Sponheim. Nel 1160, all'età di 80 anni, la sua occupazione è indicata come mönch nel Altenberg, Kreis Wetzlar, Rheinprovinz, Preußen. Morì dopo il 12 ottobre 1160, nel Altenberg, Odenthal, Rheinisch-Bergischer Kreis, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Germania, e fu sepolto a Kurfürstentum Pfalz, Heiliges Römisches Reich.

ja.lifeSummary: Graf Adolf von Berg IIIが1080年にヘイリジェズ ルoミシェス レイチハーゾグトゥム バーグで生まれた時，父Adolf II. von Berg-Hövelは35歳，母Adelheid von Laufenは31歳であった。彼はIrmgard Von Sponheimとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。1160年，80歳の時，プレウssンルヘインプロヴィンズクレイス ウエトズラーアルテンバーグでの職業はmönchと記録されている。彼は1160年10月12日以後にドイツノードルヘイン-ウエストファレンルヘイニスチ-バージッシャー クレイスオデンサルアルテンバーグで死去し，ヘイリジェズ ルoミシェス レイチクルフuルステントゥム プファルズにて埋葬された。

ko.Altena, Westfalen, Preußen, Deutschland: 독일 프레우ss 프로빈츠 웨스트파렌 크레이스 하겐 월드바우어 알텐아
ko.lifeSummary: Graf Adolf von Berg III(이)가 1080년 헤이리그스 o미슈스 라이치 허초그투 버그에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Adolf II. von Berg-Hövel(은)는 35세였고, 그의 어머니 Adelheid von Laufen(은)는 31세였다. 그는 Irmgard Von Sponheim(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 1160년, 80세의 나이로 그의 직업은 프레우ss 리인프로빈츠 크레이스 w츠라 알텐버그에서 mönch이었다. 그는 1160년 10월 12일 이후 독일 노르드하이-w스트파렌 리이니슈-버지슈어 크레이스 오덴솔 알텐버그에서 사망했으며 헤이리그스 o미슈스 라이치 커프u르스투 p팔즈에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Graf Adolf von Berg III nasceu em 1080, em Herzogtum Berg, Heiliges Römisches Reich, seu pai, Adolf II. von Berg-Hövel, tinha 35 anos e sua mãe, Adelheid von Laufen, tinha 31 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho com Irmgard Von Sponheim. Em 1160, com 80 anos, sua profissão aparece como mönch em Altenberg, Kreis Wetzlar, Rheinprovinz, Preußen. Ele faleceu após 12 de outubro de 1160, em Altenberg, Odenthal, Rheinisch-Bergischer Kreis, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Alemanha, e foi sepultado em Kurfürstentum Pfalz, Heiliges Römisches Reich.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Graf Adolf von Berg III родился в 1080 г. в местности хирзогтум бирг, хилигесь рoмищесь раич, его отцу по имени Adolf II. von Berg-Hövel было 35 лет, а матери, которую звали Adelheid von Laufen, 31. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын от Irmgard Von Sponheim. В 1160 году, в возрасте 80 лет,  он работал по профессии mönch в местности алтенбирг, крис вецлар, раинпровинз, Пруссия. Он умер после 12 октябрь 1160 г. в местности алтенбирг, одентэл, раинищ-биргищир крис, нордраин-вестфеилен, Германия и был похоронен в местности курфuрстентум пфалз, хилигесь рoмищесь раич.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Graf Adolf von Berg III nació en 1080, en Herzogtum Berg, Heiliges Römisches Reich, su papá, Adolf II. von Berg-Hövel, tenía 35 años y su mamá, Adelheid von Laufen, tenía 31 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con Irmgard Von Sponheim. En 1160, a la edad de 80 años, su ocupación registrada era mönch en Altenberg, Kreis Wetzlar, Rheinprovinz, Preußen. Murió después de 12 de octubre de 1160, en Altenberg, Odenthal, Rheinisch-Bergischer Kreis, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Alemania, y fue sepultado en Kurfürstentum Pfalz, Heiliges Römisches Reich.
